meaning: n.鍋忚,璧风敤浜叉垰,瑁欏甫鍏崇郴
examples: Cage changed his name to deflect accusations of nepotism.
Many will regard his appointment as the kind of nepotism the banking industry ought to avoid.
With Frank's father the West Ham first team coach, sections of the support there cried nepotism.
But the fight sent a clear message to other chaebol: no more nepotism as usual.
Mr Saakashvili made his name as a crusader for honest government and against nepotism.
